All right now we are going to be going over the org chart and the customer flow chart So this is essentially with the staff that you have in your team How that what they do and who they report to and who their direct reports are so this helps keep you super organized especially as you scale You are able to build a team of teams, which is really important because each one of these teams will own that core function of your business and By doing that if there's ever an issue with one of the core functions, you know exactly where to go and where to look So this is the org chart for a class F. Jim Obviously if you do not have this many team members, you would only have a few of them But it shows you ultimately where you want to build to now We break it up into four areas acquisition Then the intro period or the introduction of that new member then fulfillment. So that would be What their normal EFT experience as a member of your gym would be like and then lifetime value So how long do you keep them for and what are you doing to retain them? So the sales manager is the main person that absorbs Acquisition and we always have somebody else helping them. So it's not just a one-man show on any one of these because Having a smaller having two people working together to own one of the core functions is going to be really really important so Lead gen can be the person that is helping get all the leads for the sales manager or even nurture them in some way Shape or form and a lot of the times This will remain the owner even if you scale really high because the owners are typically the ones that are working on lead gen then You have the introduction period for anybody that gets sold by the acquisition team and that would include your challenge manager So a lot of the times when you're looking for a full-time challenge manager You want somebody that's really good at administrative stuff keeping groups organized keeping Everybody in good communication scheduling appointments making sure those are serviced so high level of organization and Administrative stuff now that doesn't always mean they're going to be the greatest at coaching as well So you could pair them with one of the coaches that you feel is a little bit stronger That is good with helping people overcome Not getting results or recovery or nutrition one of those things so they can answer all the questions that challengers might have that don't pertain to Staying organized in a meetings and appointments because that's a that's a pretty heavy task to keep 90 So challengers organized or even if it's less than that 50 challengers. It's still a lot of work. So My point is that you'll want somebody that's really good at administrative stuff to do that And it doesn't always happen to be the same type of people that's great at coaching So you put those two together and they should be able to to deliver an amazing experience to all of your challengers Then you have fulfillment. So the day-to-day, right, which is obviously your ops manager and they are at this point really doing the day-to-day of the business of all of the Tasks that include making sure the rest of the staff has everything they need to absolutely crush Their job making sure that all the systems are running that things are going out things are coming in everything is working perfectly so they'll have three direct reports they'll have the Front desk person if you have a dedicated front desk, they'll have the cleaners or the maintenance So obviously the gym needs to be cleaned and done on a regular basis now if you have a cleaning company doing that if you have a Just one person that you hired for ten dollars an hour if you have your staff doing it However, you have it doing they should typically be reporting to the ops manager same thing with maintenance I don't know if you guys have people fixing stuff I usually did it myself and fix a lot of stuff But now that I have not really working in the gym We need that we need to hire somebody if there's someone puts a hole in the wall I mean it's got to get fixed right so the ops manager would coordinate all of that stuff then They also help with the trainers or the coaches But the administrative stuff not like coaching them on how to do things I got a coach better and how to assess form and all that but they're they're helping them with vacation requests maybe payroll if they have a question about payroll or billing or They do all of the all the administrative stuff when it comes to that team They're paired with the lead trainer. So the lead trainer does all of the actual coaching and programming and all of that stuff for the coaches and really helps them with fulfillment and of What your gym provides from a workout standpoint from a fitness standpoint? So these two together are splitting your bigger bulk of coaches that you have so there's not quite as many direct reports for each one of them It turns out to be in like a half For each of them, which is totally doable then finally you'll have a retention manager and they're running the five horsemen of retention They're running all the systems for retention and again, they're they're they're very they're highly They're highly Administrative to run all these systems and keep it super organized and no one's missing a handwritten card And nobody's not getting reached out to and the events go really well But because of that you're acquiring somebody that's really an operator a really good administrator And they may not be the best when it comes to fitness or nutrition And that's why we pair them with an accountability coach again This could be another one of your trainers that you think is really good with Fitness nutrition things like that recovery assessing problems And that is somebody that the retention manager can go to and say like hey I got Susie looking to cancel but it's mostly because she's perpetually sore and doesn't like the feeling like what should I tell her? What can I do? Can you meet with her? So they're a tag team and together they Their job is retaining and really over delivering to your members So they stay as long as possible which increases the lifetime value So this is the org chart of how your your staff is being utilized and how they work together and Each one of these managers would report to the owner So the owner has four direct reports and then the managers have just one person they're working with unless it's the ops and the lead trainer They're tag teaming the rest of the staff. So that is the org chart And that is what it looks like at class F You would simply just be missing some of these pieces If you don't have all that staff yet now, we'll go right into how these How these roles play into the customer journey? All right, so we're gonna go to the customer flow chart and when they touch each one of these Roles and different touch points they have with your business in general. So it kind of starts like this Let's start off with the owner the owner is typically doing Lee Jen right so even though they're not really meeting the owner, maybe they're seeing the owner in the ad Or they're seeing them on the website or however you do it but typically the owner is the first person that ever reaches out to the the lead before they become a member and It's through advertising then they come into the gym and the first person they meet is the sales manager So the sales manager And I mean like the first person they actually work with like maybe they meet the front desk person first I don't know but the sales manager is who they go to and that is the person that will close them on a mid-ticket challenge And get them signed up to do the challenge So after that the next representative of your business that they see would be the trainers or the accountability coaches Whoever is facilitating your WAN days? so Taking before pictures Explaining the nutrition to them selling them supplements Again, if it's somebody else then you just have that circle there, but for us we have our trainers do it And they they really help them Get onboarded correctly and get them super fired up to do this challenge The next person they begin working with is the challenge manager So now the challenge manager is working with them throughout the next six weeks And really helping them get the most the best results Overdelivering on the experience checking with them every week You guys know so the challenge manager is really working with them over over that six week period And then finally after that's done we convert them over to an EFT and then they begin working with the retention manager And the coaches so these two together are Going to be fulfilling them indefinitely and really increasing that lifetime value for as long as they can Six months eight months nine months 12 months two years four years I mean the the better you can fulfill the longer you can keep Your members and so the retention manager's only job is to do that along with your trainers and coaches who are actually doing the Fulfilling the actual workouts and all of that stuff as you can see the ops manager is not in here because typically the ops manager doesn't have a specific responsibility with the clients themselves, so They certainly meet the clients and they certainly talk with them and they're a big presence at the gym But they don't have a specific function or a role that needs to be serviced that is customer facing Typically they are there to make sure all of these people have exactly what they need to dominate and crush and that every system is in place And is being done correctly, right? So they are running the systems and that doesn't mean like working directly with the clients one-on-one So that is the customer flow experience And together going over that in New York chart You should have a really good idea of how your business is operating from the customer's point of view
